bugs.error_message: Deploy preflight takes 5-6 min per invocation; PHP lint walks vendor/
bugs.root_cause: find at line 206 walked all .php under THEME_DIR with no prunes. 3,881 files total but only 124 are the theme's; the other 3,756 are Composer vendor deps. At 0.36s per php -l, vendor-lint alone cost 5-6 min per deploy. vendor/ was already gitignored, excluded from tarball, and excluded from phpcs — the lint walk was the last path that didn't get the memo.
bugs.fix: Added -prune for vendor, node_modules, tests, .git. Replaced hardcoded /opt/homebrew/bin/php with `command -v php` fallback. Added file count to success log. Scope: 124 (was 3,881). Expected preflight: ~45s (was ~5-6 min).

bugs.error_message: No concurrency guard; two deploys can race on the remote .old.$swap_id rename
bugs.root_cause: Hot-swap uses a timestamp+PID swap_id for rename-aside-and-rename-into-place. Two parallel deploys produce different swap_ids but race on the same live path. If deploy B's rename lands between A's rename-aside and rename-into-place, A loses its backup anchor and B's backup points at A's half-extracted tarball.
bugs.fix: Added acquire_deploy_lock() using /tmp/skyyrose-deploy.lock with PID stored inside. Stale lock (PID dead) reclaimed; live lock refuses second deploy with exit 2. release_deploy_lock fires in cleanup trap.

bugs.error_message: If verify_live() fails, site stays broken; no auto-rollback
bugs.root_cause: verify_live returned non-zero but main() only logged and exited 1 — the broken theme remained live. Old theme was already deleted by the immediate rm -rf .old.$swap_id at end of try_rsync, so no backup to restore.
bugs.fix: Added auto_rollback() that SSHs remote, reverses the swap (mv broken to .failed.$ts, restore .old.$ts into live path), and flushes caches. Changed try_rsync to NOT delete .old.$ts immediately. Cleanup trap inspects ROLLBACK_REQUESTED and calls auto_rollback on verify failure. Exit code 3 signals rollback occurred.

bugs.error_message: .old.$swap_id deleted immediately after swap; no rollback anchor
bugs.root_cause: Original compound command chained `mv new -> live && rm -rf .old.$swap_id`. Bounded disk but destroyed the only artifact auto-rollback could restore from.
bugs.fix: Removed the immediate rm. Added deterministic retention: after every swap, ls .old.* on remote and xargs rm -rf all but the 2 most recent. Bounded disk, rollback anchor preserved.

bugs.error_message: verify_live checked only homepage; CSS/JS breakage silent when WP-CDN cached HTML
bugs.root_cause: verify_live() only curled the homepage. If WP-CDN served cached HTML but new CSS/JS 404'd from origin, check passed while site was visibly broken. Also: no per-phase timing, no structured log.
bugs.fix: Extended verify_live() to curl style.css, immersive.css, immersive.js with cache-busting. Every asset URL must return 200. Added DEPLOY_CHECK_ASSETS env var for project-specific additions. Added phase_start/phase_end/phase_summary (bash 3.2 compatible scalars — no associative arrays). Tee'd stdout+stderr to /tmp/skyyrose-deploy-<ts>.log.

bugs.error_message: tar -czf wastes CPU compressing already-compressed media (WebP/GLB/JPG/PNG)
bugs.root_cause: Theme payload ~380 MB, of which ~316 MB is already-compressed binary media. gzip achieves ~0% reduction on those formats but costs single-threaded CPU per deploy.
bugs.fix: Runtime-detect tar --zstd and zstd binary. When available, use `tar --zstd -cf` (5x faster, similar ratio on text parts). Fallback to plain `-cf` (no compression) when zstd absent — still faster than gzip on pre-compressed media. Matching remote extract flag. Tarball extension adjusts.

bugs.error_message: SSHPASS exported for entire deploy duration, leaking credential to every child process
bugs.root_cause: build_ssh_cmd() unconditionally exported SSHPASS as soon as the script started; remained in env for preflight, tar, every subprocess until exit. On shared systems, `ps e <pid>` or /proc/<pid>/environ leak the password.
bugs.fix: Moved SSHPASS export from build_ssh_cmd() into try_rsync() just before scp. Added `unset SSHPASS` immediately after remote extract. Exposure window shrunk from entire deploy (~7 min) to sub-second. No functional change — sshpass -e reads from env at invocation.

bugs.error_message: 43.4 MB orphan GIF bloating every deploy payload for no reason
bugs.root_cause: GIF (1920x1080, 326 frames) superseded by rose-gold-spin.mp4 (3.86 MB) and rose-gold-spin.webm (2.34 MB) on 2026-04-14 but source GIF never removed. Zero references anywhere in theme PHP/CSS/JS/JSON. Every deploy paid the tar + upload cost for a file that would never be served.
bugs.fix: Pre-delete grep confirmed zero references. Deleted the .gif. MP4/WebM remain as modern animated-logo assets. Savings: 43.4 MB off every future tarball (11% payload reduction).

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: DEPLOY_LOCK_FD should be DEPLOY_LOCK_HELD

bugs.root_cause: value: 'lh-004', label: 'Love Hurts Varsity Jacket', collection: 'love-hurts' → 'lh-006', label: 'The Fannie', collection: 'love-hurts'
bugs.fix: Changed value: 'lh-004', label: 'Love Hurts Varsity Jacket', collection: 'love-hurts' → 'lh-006', label: 'The Fannie', collection: 'love-hurts'

bugs.error_message: front-page.php uses wc_get_products() to count products per collection — breaks when WooCommerce categories are not synced to CSV catalog
bugs.root_cause: wc_get_products() queries WC product categories, which are separate from the CSV catalog. Category sync is not guaranteed. Result: count is 0 when WC categories don't exist.
bugs.fix: Replaced wc_get_products() block with count(skyyrose_get_collection_products($col['slug'])) — reads directly from CSV catalog helper

bugs.error_message: template-preorder-gateway.php: (a) missing kids-capsule collection, (b) $fallback_products uses nonexistent SKUs (br-hoodie, lh-hoodie, sg-hoodie etc.), (c) wc_get_products() query with fallback to fake data, (d) card args missing image_url and correct permalink
bugs.root_cause: Template was written before CSV catalog helpers existed. Fallback products were placeholder data with invented SKUs never in the catalog. kids-capsule was simply omitted. WC_Product branch was dead code once WC categories weren't populated.
bugs.fix: Removed $fallback_products array and wc_get_products() block entirely. Added kids-capsule to $collections. Replaced product loading with skyyrose_get_collection_products() per slug. Fixed card args: image_url uses skyyrose_product_image_uri(front_model_image|image), permalink uses skyyrose_product_url($sku), price uses skyyrose_format_price($item). Removed WC_Product instanceof branch (catalog always returns arrays).

bugs.error_message: TypeError: e.target.closest is not a function — SVG elements and other non-Element nodes dispatched to document-level event listeners don't have .closest()
bugs.root_cause: SVG child elements (path, circle, use, etc.) are SVGElement not HTMLElement and may not implement .closest() in older browsers. document-level capture listeners receive events from any element on the page including SVG internals.
bugs.fix: Added typeof guard before every .closest() call: getCardData(), mouseenter listener, mouseleave listener, click listener — each now checks !el || typeof el.closest !== 'function' before calling.

bugs.error_message: 404.php references skyyrose-mascot-reference.png which does not exist in assets/images/mascot/ — only skyy-canonical.jpeg exists
bugs.root_cause: 404.php used an old filename (skyyrose-mascot-reference.png) that was never created. The canonical mascot file is skyy-canonical.jpeg.
bugs.fix: Replaced both references (path and URL) to skyyrose-mascot-reference.png with skyy-canonical.jpeg in the mascot fallback block of 404.php.

bugs.error_message: [Resolved] Voyage free-tier rate limit (10K TPM, 3 RPM) blocked full catalog indexing
bugs.root_cause: No payment method on Voyage account → free-tier throttle. Catalog (~32K tokens) blew the 10K TPM cap.
bugs.fix: Added payment method on https://dashboard.voyageai.com/. Standard rate limits unlocked. 33-SKU catalog indexes in 1.60s. 200M free tokens still apply.

bugs.error_message: Tripo generate_multiview_image hallucinated brand canon on 30 SKUs (120 renders) — invented 'rose-on-cloud' motif on br-001, rendered br-011 hockey jersey as cyan/teal hoodie with invented crest, applied off-canon patch to wrong location on sg-007 signature beanie
bugs.root_cause: Tripo `generate_multiview_image` template runs FLUX.1 Kontext with naked image-only input — accepts NO prompt, NO logo overlay, NO dossier branding spec, NO palette token. With no canon anchor, FLUX defaults to training-set priors. Dispatch boundary had no guard distinguishing branded SKUs (which Tripo cannot render correctly) from unbranded clean tech-flats (which it can). Additionally, SKUs with empty catalog `image` column silently fell back to `front_model_image` (a model-on shot), which the template re-builds from scratch — changing garment type entirely (jersey → hoodie). No QA gate caught the regression; STOP-AND-SHOW manifest showed cost only, no output preview.
bugs.fix: scripts/tripo_dispatch.py — added classify_skus() function that blocks at the dispatch boundary: (1) SKUs whose dossier frontmatter has `logo_reference:` populated are blocked as BRANDED with directive to route via ADK render_pipeline. (2) SKUs with empty `image` column or missing tech-flat file are blocked as NO TECH-FLAT. --force-branded escape hatch added with WARNING and still-required explicit y to dispatch. Per-SKU block reason printed in manifest before y/N prompt. 12 unit tests in tests/test_tripo_dispatch_guards.py covering both guards plus escape hatch. renders/output/tripo/QUARANTINE.md written marking all 120 existing outputs as discard-do-not-publish. Cerebrum Do-Not-Repeat entry added 2026-05-11.

bugs.error_message: CURS-03: luxury cursor JS loads on immersive templates (slug='immersive') — no exclusion gate in enqueue.php
bugs.root_cause: wp_enqueue_script('skyyrose-luxury-cursor', ...) lives inside skyyrose_enqueue_global_scripts() at lines 249-259 with no conditional check. Template slug detection exists (skyyrose_get_current_template_slug() maps template-immersive-*.php to 'immersive') but is not consulted before cursor JS enqueue. Result: cursor JS loads on all pages including immersive templates where the cursor is CSS-hidden.
bugs.fix: FIXED in theme 1.1.2 (commit 016f7025f, deployed 2026-05-12). wordpress-theme/skyyrose-flagship/inc/enqueue.php lines 247-263: wrapped the luxury-cursor wp_enqueue_script() block in `if ('immersive' !== skyyrose_get_current_template_slug())`. The template slug helper was already available in the same file (no new dependency). After deploy, live verification confirmed zero <script> tags loading luxury-cursor.js on /experience-black-rose/. Regression test tests/test_luxury_cursor.py::test_cursor_not_loaded_on_immersive flipped from intentional-fail to pass (3/3 green).

bugs.error_message: DATA-01: /collection-black-rose/, /collection-love-hurts/, /collection-signature/, /collection-kids-capsule/ all returned HTTP 200 but served the homepage (page-id-9822, page-template-front-page-php) instead of the matching template-collection-*.php. Collection pages were absent from the WordPress DB.
bugs.root_cause: skyyrose_run_activation_setup() runs once via add_action('init', ...) at priority 30, gated by `if get_option('skyyrose_activation_setup_version') === SKYYROSE_SETUP_VERSION return;`. The stored option was '4.0.0', matching the constant, so the init runner short-circuited on every request. However, the four collection pages had been deleted from the WP admin at some point (or never created on this site's initial activation), so skyyrose_create_required_pages() never re-ran to recreate them. wp_safe_redirect logic in inc/redirects.php only handled legacy /collections/{slug}/ -> /collection-{slug}/ rewrites — it assumed the canonical destination pages existed, which they did not.
bugs.fix: Bumped SKYYROSE_SETUP_VERSION constant from '4.0.0' to '4.1.0' in inc/theme-activation-setup.php (commit 016f7025f, theme 1.1.2). On next request after deploy the init runner sees the version mismatch, fires skyyrose_run_activation_setup(), which calls skyyrose_create_required_pages(). That function iterates the slug map (already includes the four collection slugs at lines 69-88) and runs wp_insert_post() for any slug returned as missing by get_page_by_path(), assigning _wp_page_template meta to point at the matching template-collection-*.php. Post-deploy verified live: /collection-black-rose/ = page-id-9454, love-hurts=9455, signature=9456, kids-capsule=9651 — all routing to their correct templates.
bugs.regression_test: scripts/verify_live_structure.py COLLECTION_HERO_ASSETS asserts img[src*='sr-collection-*.webp'] per collection

bugs.error_message: Stop hook error: Hook JSON output validation failed — (root): Invalid input
bugs.root_cause: Stop hook emitted {hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext: ...} JSON. That schema is valid only for UserPromptSubmit and PostToolUse. Stop accepts only {decision, reason, continue, stopReason, suppressOutput, systemMessage}. Claude Code schema validator rejected the unknown root-level shape.
bugs.fix: Replaced jq stdout JSON emission with stderr printf. Stop fires after Claude finishes, so additionalContext injection is moot — the reminder is for the human, stderr is the right channel. Stdout now empty, schema validator passes. Marker file deletion preserved.

bugs.error_message: NOT SUPPORTED: option missingRefs. Pass empty schema with $id that should be ignored to ajv.addSchema. TypeError: Cannot set properties of undefined (setting 'defaultMeta') at ajvOrig
bugs.root_cause: ESLint 9.39.3 bundles ajv that rejects the 'missingRefs' option used by @eslint/eslintrc's legacy config loader. Lint-staged invokes ESLint 9 which loads eslintrc-universal.cjs which calls ajvOrig with NOT-SUPPORTED options -> hard crash before any file is linted. Blocks pre-commit on any staged .ts/.tsx under frontend/**
bugs.fix: Scoped npm overrides in root package.json: 'eslint': {'ajv': '^6.14.0'} AND '@eslint/eslintrc': {'ajv': '^6.14.0'}. Forces both packages to nest ajv v6.15.0 in their own node_modules while keeping top-level ajv@8.18.0 for other consumers. Verified: 'cd frontend && npx eslint <file>' runs to completion (2 warnings, 0 errors, exit 0). Fixed in chore/p1-p5-backlog at TBD-commit.

bugs.error_message: error: 'tuple' object is not callable (TestModules in tests/aos/shell/test_commands.py)
bugs.root_cause: ModuleRegistry.manifests and .registered_types are @property accessors, not methods; _cmd_modules called them with () which invoked the returned tuple/frozenset.
bugs.fix: Dropped the parentheses: kernel.modules.manifests / kernel.modules.registered_types accessed as properties.

bugs.error_message: Phase 14 INFRA-01 regression: scripts/nano_banana/catalog.py bypasses skyyrose.core.catalog_loader (independent csv.DictReader) — single-source-of-truth contract broken; Plan 14-02 SUMMARY claim false
bugs.root_cause: Plan 14-02 added a catalog_loader shim (commit 51dc3ff8b). Later commits a22074ab3 and 8737e3714 overwrote scripts/nano_banana/catalog.py with a standalone csv.DictReader parser (lines 8, 26-55), regressing INFRA-01. Data stays consistent (same canonical CSV file) but the three pipelines no longer share the catalog adapter — the contract, not the data, is violated.
bugs.fix: Option A applied. Rewrote scripts/nano_banana/catalog.py load_catalog() + module docstring + CATALOG_CSV constant to source rows via skyyrose.core.catalog_loader.read_catalog_rows / bool_col / CATALOG_CSV (env-overridable). Removed the local csv.DictReader. Preserved the richer HEAD functions untouched (find_source_image, find_back_source, load_products, load_specs, get_material_spec — load-bearing per cli.py/produce_async.py) and kept engine_override + back_source_override; added garment_type from garment_type_lock (INFRA-04 alignment). Added CI regression gate tests/test_catalog_csv_integrity.py::test_nano_banana_catalog_routes_through_core_loader asserting nb.read_catalog_rows IS core.read_catalog_rows, canonical CATALOG_CSV, and no csv.DictReader in load_catalog. Verified: 21/21 integrity tests green incl. test_python_loaders_agree_on_sku_set; nano+elite agree on 33 SKUs; black/ruff/isort clean.

bugs.error_message: 5 prod modules reference data/product-catalog.csv as 'single source of truth' but that file is NOT on the main tree and NOT git-tracked (only the canonical wordpress-theme/skyyrose-flagship/data/skyyrose-catalog.csv is tracked)
bugs.root_cause: Legacy second-CSV references predating the catalog_loader consolidation. Affected: database/seed_catalog.py, agents/social_media_agent.py, scripts/nano-banana-vton.py, agents/claude_sdk/domain_agents/content.py, renders/preflight.py (comments). They point at a phantom data/product-catalog.csv path.
bugs.fix: PENDING — OUT OF bug-102/Phase 14 scope (would touch DB seeding + social agent + vton, unrelated subsystems — scope-creep risk). Handoff target for the deferred v1.3 Site Fix milestone's first task (audit CSV -> one canonical source). Do NOT fix inside Phase 14.

bugs.error_message: (eval):17: PIPESTATUS[0]: parameter not set  — verification harness for devworkflows scripts failed under set -u
bugs.file: scripts/devworkflows/ (verification harness, not the scripts themselves)
bugs.root_cause: The Bash tool executes commands via zsh (env Shell: zsh), not bash. PIPESTATUS is a bash-only array; zsh exposes pipe exit codes as the lowercase 1-indexed `pipestatus` array. With `set -u`, referencing the unset PIPESTATUS made the harness abort. The 3 devworkflow scripts were unaffected (they run under their own `#!/usr/bin/env bash` shebang) — only the inline test harness tripped.
bugs.fix: In Bash-tool one-liners, never use ${PIPESTATUS[*]}. Capture exit status directly: run the command, then `rc=$?` on the next line (no pipe), or redirect to a file and read $? immediately. Reserve PIPESTATUS for code inside `bash script.sh` files.

bugs.error_message: br-011 deep-dive RCA revealed 3 drift gaps in tripo guard + catalog/dossier alignment + QUARANTINE.md mischaracterization (post-bug-096 hardening)
bugs.root_cause: (1) classify_skus only checked tech-flat file existence — ignored authoritative catalog render_is_tech_flat column. br-011 had a 12.6MB PNG flagged 0 (branded product render). --force-branded would have approved it. (2) QUARANTINE.md row 24 mischaracterized br-011 as NHL Authentic cut, no hood, mesh side panels — dossier says Hockey-style pullover hoodie. Garment shape rendered correct; only branding hallucinated. (3) Catalog garment_type_lock=jersey conflicted with dossier hoodie construction.
bugs.fix: Added _is_catalog_tech_flat() helper and inserted middle guard layer in classify_skus: BRANDED -> CATALOG TECH-FLAT FLAG -> FILE EXISTS. Updated 4 existing tests + added 3 new (total 16/16). Catalog br-011 garment_type_lock: jersey -> hoodie. QUARANTINE.md row 24 corrected + Fix section bumped to 4 guards + test count 12 -> 16.

bugs.error_message: AttributeError: property 'name' of 'ReplicateProvider' object has no setter (tests/test_p0_ssrf_replicate.py fixture)
bugs.root_cause: Fixture used ReplicateProvider.__new__ then assigned p.name, but name is a read-only @property returning a constant — assignment never worked; tests were committed without a green run
bugs.fix: Removed the p.name assignment (property already returns 'replicate'); 20/20 pass

bugs.error_message: Founder review: sg-006/sg-014 renders showed 'WRONG PRODUCT — rendered the signature windbreaker outfit'; 41 of 79 renders flagged total
bugs.root_cause: sg-006/sg-014 dossiers describe a white chevron zip-up garment (the windbreaker-set design) instead of the mint pullover/sweats shown in their techflats — dossier was Claude-authored (cmem #11235) from wrong reference photos; its reference_image field points at a mislabeled file that is actually the black sherpa jacket. gpt-image-2 obeyed dossier text over the reference image.
bugs.fix: sg-006/sg-014 added to config.EXCLUDED_SKUS until founder re-authors dossiers; founder verbatim corrections from review board injected into prompts via data/render-corrections.json; new photorealistic_not_flat QC gate; MATERIAL/PHOTOREALISM/BRANDING-IS-EXHAUSTIVE prompt directives

bugs.error_message: PR #540 merged missing its branch tip: push used 409187921:feat/oai-render-pipeline assuming 2b2ab382a was the parent, but 2b2ab382a (paired-looks skip fix) was the CHILD/tip — stranded after merge
bugs.root_cause: Commit order assumption inverted: standup notes listed '409187921 + 2b2ab382a' and executor verified the range existed but not the parent-child direction; pushing the older SHA silently excluded the tip
bugs.fix: Recovery PR #544 pushed 2b2ab382a as fix/oai-render-paired-looks-skip, 52/52 pipeline tests green, merged 0f0ea0c43. Lesson: push BRANCH TIPS or verify with `git log -1 --format=%P <sha>` before sha:branch pushes; post-merge gate `merge-base --is-ancestor <every-sha> origin/main` caught it

bugs.error_message: Uncaught SyntaxError: Identifier 'ASSET_PREFIX' has already been declared — all v2 JS dead, dock/grid never populated
bugs.root_cause: Two top-level scripts on one page both declared const ASSET_PREFIX in shared global scope
bugs.fix: Wrapped both files in IIFEs; skill updated to require IIFE wrapping in multi-script briefs

bugs.error_message: v2 dock rendered default rose-gold accent instead of Black Rose silver
bugs.root_cause: data-collection attribute on <main> but dock element sits after </main> — token override never reached it
bugs.fix: Moved data-collection to <body> so all descendants inherit collection tokens

bugs.error_message: Black Rose script logotype invisible on #0A0A0A background (v2 + v5 heroes)
bugs.root_cause: br-brand-script-logotype is black lettering; brightness filters cannot lift pure black pixels
bugs.fix: Scoped [data-collection=black-rose] .lp-hero__lockup { filter: invert(1) grayscale(1) } — silver-white matches BR register

bugs.error_message: v5 announcement bar showed internal canon text 'No countdown timers. No fake scarcity.' as customer-facing copy
bugs.root_cause: Builder agent leaked founder-canon guardrail instructions into UI copy
bugs.fix: Stripped to shipping fact only; skill updated: internal guardrails never appear as customer copy

bugs.error_message: Delivered prototype HTML files rendered unstyled skeletons when downloaded — all assets referenced via relative paths escaping into the repo tree
bugs.root_cause: Skill output contract allowed multi-file pages with cross-tree relative asset paths; 'verified in place' hid the escape
bugs.fix: build-standalone.py inlines CSS/JS + embeds all images/fonts as data URIs + MutationObserver shim resolves adapter-built paths; skill now mandates single-file locked-and-loaded delivery + isolated-copy verification

bugs.error_message: v2 referenced images/products/jersey-last-oakland-baseball/<file> — subdirectory holds only collection-card images, front-model file is flat
bugs.root_cause: Builder invented per-product-subdir paths instead of using brief's verified flat paths
bugs.fix: Normalized 3 subdir refs to flat verified filenames; rebuilt standalone

bugs.error_message: Signature Sherpa render (red-rose closed jacket) mis-filed as black-rose-sherpa-jacket/black-rose-sherpa-jacket-front-model.webp — surfaced as 'Signature Sherpa in the Black Rose Collection' on v2 mockup; founder flagged
bugs.root_cause: Render output saved under wrong product's subdir during earlier render pipeline runs; filename treated as identity without pixel verification
bugs.fix: git mv to signature-sherpa-jacket-front-closed.{webp,avif}; CSV sg-009 front_model_image -> new file (upgrade from ghost); br-006 confirmed correct on flat hooded render; CLAUDE.md learning + cerebrum entries added

bugs.error_message: Flagship builder shipped 'Free shipping over $100' (canon: $200+), type-rendered collection names on gateway panels, raw white-diamond seal
bugs.root_cause: Shipping threshold not in dispatch brief's canon block; panel name rule ambiguity; patch art has white square background
bugs.fix: Taste-gate caught all three: $200+ corrected, po-panel__name removed (lockup IS the name), seal circle-cropped. Skill Art Direction Standard + taste gate now codified

bugs.error_message: AssertionError: Status code 204 must not have a response body — app import crashed at api/v1/rag_anything.py:201, collected by new Stop-hook pytest run
bugs.root_cause: PEP 563 (`from __future__ import annotations`) stringifies the handler's `-> None` return annotation; FastAPI 0.115 get_type_hints resolves it to the NoneType CLASS, which is truthy as response_model, tripping the 204-no-body assert at route registration. Only route in repo combining future-import + `-> None` + 204.
bugs.fix: Explicit `response_model=None` on the @router.delete decorator. 4 sibling 204 routes (elite_studio, v2/assets, v2/creative, v2/webhooks) have future-import but UNannotated handlers — dormant; adding `-> None` to them without response_model=None will re-trigger this.
